subject: Databases
title: DISTINCT Removes Duplicates
description: A fast breakdown of DISTINCT Removes Duplicates for builders.

hook.text: You want every unique country in your users table. SELECT DISTINCT country FROM users. Done.

buildup.text: DISTINCT scans all matching rows and removes duplicates from the result set.

discovery.text: It works on multiple columns: SELECT DISTINCT city, country returns unique city-country pairs.

twist.text: DISTINCT can be slow on large tables. It sorts or hashes all results to find duplicates.

climax.text: Sometimes GROUP BY is a better alternative. It lets you aggregate while removing duplicates.

punchline.text: DISTINCT cuts duplicates. Use it when you need unique values.

quiz.question: When might GROUP BY be preferred over DISTINCT?
options: When you want to aggregate data like counting or summing while removing duplicates
GROUP BY is always faster than DISTINCT
DISTINCT doesn't work with text columns
